author: chatgpt-codex-connector
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Harden third-party Actions before teaching CI**

This recommends marketplace Actions as copy-paste CI building blocks, but the gift repo will be beginner- and Claude-edited, so it needs the missing workflow hardening rule: pin third-party actions to full commit SHAs and set minimal `permissions:` before any render/compile workflow is shipped. GitHub recommends SHA pinning to limit compromised dependencies, and a linter such as Ensure SHA Pinned Actions can enforce it ([GitHub changelog](https://github.blog/changelog/2025-08-15-github-actions-policy-now-supports-blocking-and-sha-pinning-actions/), [marketplace action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/ensure-sha-pinned-actions)).

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
